prompt:
=== FARSHORE — RULES PRIMER ===

YOUR ROLE
  You command at the strategic level. You form task forces and give each an
  objective ("Task Force 1: secure the eastern sea"). You do NOT move
  individual units, choose individual targets, or resolve combat — subordinate
  officers carry out your intent and report back. If you find yourself naming a
  unit's destination tile, you've dropped too low.

VICTORY
  You win when the enemy owns zero cities (a player with no cities cannot build
  and is finished). Only cities build. A neutral city can be captured by an
  army like any other city; it then builds for you — taking neutrals does not
  win the game, but each one adds production.

UNITS
  name        moves-on  HP  spd  str  carries      builds-in
  ARMY        land       1    1    1   —             5 turns
  FIGHTER     air        1    8    1   —            10   (range 20; must refuel)
  PATROL      sea        2    4    1   —            15
  DESTROYER   sea        3    3    2   —            20
  SUBMARINE   sea        2    2    3   —            25
  TRANSPORT   sea        3    2    0   6 armies     30   (str 0: cannot fight)
  CARRIER     sea        8    2    1   8 fighters   40
  BATTLESHIP  sea       18    2    4   —            50
  SATELLITE   orbit      1    1    0   —            50   (recon only; cannot attack or be attacked; 50-turn life)

MOVEMENT & TERRAIN
  - Land units move on land + cities; they cannot enter water.
  - Sea units move on water + cities; they cannot cross land, and must sail
    around it. Cities are the only tiles where land and sea units meet.
  - A unit moves up to its speed at full HP; damaged units move less. A unit
    regains HP only on a turn it does not move.
  - One unit per tile (exception: cargo, below).

COMBAT  (the engine resolves it; you only weigh it)
  Probabilistic. Higher strength and higher current HP win more often; either
  side can lose HP or be destroyed. Strength-0 units (transport, satellite)
  never fight — a transport caught by an enemy warship is destroyed.

SEA TRANSPORT  (how armies cross water — and what is expected of you)
  An army can never enter water. Transports carry them: a transport loads up
  to 6 armies at a city or coastal tile, sails, and unloads onto adjacent
  land. YOUR OFFICERS DO ALL OF THIS. If a task force contains armies and a
  transport and you give it an objective across water, the officers march
  the armies to the transport, load, sail, escort, unload, and press the
  objective — you never order the rendezvous, the loading, or the landing.
  Expected use: put the armies, the transport, and (if the sea is not safe)
  a warship into ONE task force and name the objective. A transport has
  strength 0 — alone it is a target, not a warship; a warship in the same
  task force escorts it automatically. Never STAGE land units on water.
  Cargo is kind-locked — transports carry armies only, carriers carry
  fighters only; ships never carry ships. If a loaded transport or carrier
  is destroyed, everything aboard is lost with it.

CAPTURING CITIES
  Only an army captures a city, by entering it. Capture is a 50% roll, and a
  successful capture consumes the army (it garrisons the city). A city can take
  several armies to secure, and each attempt spends one.

PRODUCTION
  No currency. A city builds ONE unit at a time; it finishes after that unit's
  build-time in turns, then starts the next. Switching what a city builds
  DISCARDS all accumulated progress — effort toward one unit does not transfer
  to another.

AIR & SPECIAL
  - Fighter: must reach a FRIENDLY city or a carrier within its range (20) or
    it is lost. It cannot land on an enemy city.
  - Satellite: reveals everything within scan radius 10 — terrain AND any enemy
    units currently there — but far-reaching and deep in enemy territory. It
    only sees; no other effect, cannot be shot down.

VISION / FOG
  You see within any of your units'/cities' scan radius (army 2, sub 1,
  patrol/destroyer/battleship 3, transport 2, carrier 4, fighter 5, sat 10).
  You remember terrain you've seen, but an enemy unit is known only where you
  can see right now — remembered enemy positions may be stale.

STANDING DOCTRINE  (tested — lessons your service has already paid for)
  - A sound standing order beats a new plan. Amend only what events have
    made wrong; re-planning a working campaign is how campaigns die.
  - Meet surprises with uncommitted reserves first. A committed task
    force pulled off its mission costs you two missions.
  - A transport in waters where an enemy warship may operate sails with
    a warship in its task force, or it waits.
  - Capture attempts consume armies, and attempts fail. Assault with
    spares, never with exactly enough.
  - A staged force exists to launch. When the condition you named
    arrives, go — staging past your own trigger is production wasted.
  - Cities are your only source of force. Match what each builds to the
    campaign's next phase, not its last one.

=== ORDERS CONTRACT ===

Your orders are AMENDMENTS to the standing task forces. Output ONLY lines in
these forms — no other prose, headers, or commentary:

  TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>
  TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>
  TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>
  FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>

Every standing TF gets exactly one line; a REINFORCE or DISBAND line IS that
TF's one line. REINFORCE keeps the TF's objective and adds the listed
UNASSIGNED units to it. To change a TF's objective, RETASK it (members kept).
To rebuild membership from scratch, DISBAND (survivors return to UNASSIGNED)
and FORM anew; FORM lines are additional lines creating new TFs. UNASSIGNED
units enter play only through FORM or REINFORCE. A BUILD line is optional per
city — no BUILD line means the city keeps its current build (changing
discards accumulated work). VERB is one of:
  CAPTURE <city (x,y)> · DEFEND <city (x,y)> · SCOUT <(x,y) or compass> ·
  PATROL <(x,y) or compass> · STAGE <(x,y)>
A warship grouped with a transport escorts it — there is no ESCORT order.

CURRENT TASKINGS  (standing orders; your stated reason in quotes;
  events since, as reported; members are in UNITS below)
  TF-2  formed t38 · DEFEND (2,0) — "keep the capital garrisoned"
    since: garrisoned; no contact; no losses
  TF-5  formed t54 · CAPTURE (11,1) — "strike east and take the enemy cities with full lift and escort"
    since: loaded t55; crossed without contact; LANDED t56 on the enemy
      shore, six armies ashore (10,1)-(12,3); transports empty offshore;
      an enemy army garrisons (11,1); no losses

MAP  legend: . land  ~ water  ? fog   O my city  E enemy city  N neutral city
     a-q = your units, see UNITS (a unit inside a city shows as the city)
     X = enemy units in sight NOW (see KNOWN ENEMY)
 r0  . i O j . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r1  . k b . O . ~ ~ ~ o c E ? ?
 r2  . O . . . . ~ ~ ~ p d E g ?
 r3  . . . . O . ~ ~ ~ q e f h ?
 r4  . . . m . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r5  ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?

UNITS  (map marker · id · kind · position · tasking) — this is your
ENTIRE force; you have NOTHING else
  a  #1   army       (2,0) in city                TF-2
  b  #2   army       (2,1)                        TF-2
  c  #3   army       (10,1)                       TF-5
  d  #4   army       (10,2)                       TF-5
  e  #5   army       (10,3)                       TF-5
  f  #6   army       (11,3)                       TF-5
  g  #7   army       (12,2)                       TF-5
  h  #8   army       (12,3)                       TF-5
  i  #11  army       (1,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  j  #12  army       (3,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  k  #13  army       (1,1)                        UNASSIGNED
  l  #15  army       (4,3) in city                UNASSIGNED
  m  #17  army       (3,4)                        UNASSIGNED
  n  #18  army       (2,0) in city                UNASSIGNED
  o  #9   transport  (9,1) empty                  TF-5
  p  #16  transport  (9,2) empty                  TF-5
  q  #10  destroyer  (9,3) escorting              TF-5
MY CITIES
  (2,0) building ARMY, 4 turns left
  (1,2) building TRANSPORT, 28 turns left
  (4,3) building ARMY, 1 turn left
  (4,1) building TRANSPORT, 25 turns left (captured t51)
NEUTRAL CITIES  none known
KNOWN ENEMY
     city (11,2)
  X  enemy army garrisoning city (11,1), seen t56

It is TURN 56. Issue your amendment orders now — ONLY the line
forms defined in the ORDERS CONTRACT above.

message.content:
TF-2: REINFORCE UNITS n | Consolidate capital defense
TF-5: CONTINUE | Maintain assault on enemy city (11,1)
message.reasoning_content:
Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   **Role:** High-level commander (FARSHORE). I issue strategic directives, not tactical micro-management (moving specific units to tiles).
    *   **Context:** Turn 56 of a simulation game ("Farshore").
    *   **Goal:** Win by eliminating enemy cities. Only build cities for production/victory points.
    *   **Input:** Game state including rules, standing taskings, map legend, current units/cities/production status.
    *   **Output Constraint:** ONLY the specific line formats defined in the "ORDERS CONTRACT". No prose, no commentary.

2.  **Analyze the Current State (Turn 56):**
    *   **TF-2:** Defending capital at (2,0). Garrisoned there with armies #1 & #2 (actually #2 is listed as TF-2 but map shows b at (2,1) and a at (2,0)).
        *   Wait, looking at UNIT list: `a` (#1 army) is `(2,0)` in city TF-2. `b` (#2 army) is `(2,1)`. Map shows `(2,0)` has city. Wait, map legend says "O my city".
        *   Let's check Map Legend vs Unit List consistency.
            *   Map r0: `. i O j . .` -> City at (0,0)? No, map indices are rows/cols. Let's trace carefully.
            *   Map `r0`: `. i O j . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?`. 'O' is my city. It looks like column index 2 is the city. Wait, row 0 col 2 is 'O'. So (2,0) is my capital.
            *   Units: `a` (#1 army) `(2,0)` in city TF-2. Correct.
            *   `b` (#2 army) `(2,1)`. This is adjacent to the city. But TF-2 objective is "DEFEND (2,0)". `b` needs to be retasked or just remain there as defense depth? The contract says "Every standing TF gets exactly one line". TF-2 currently has no mission in my output history? Wait, I need to see the *previous* status.
            *   The prompt says "CURRENT TASKINGS (standing orders...)". It lists: `TF-2 formed t38 · DEFEND (2,0)`. So TF-2 is already established with that tasking. I must issue an amendment line for every standing TF?
            *   Constraint: "Every standing TF gets exactly one line; a REINFORCE or DISBAND line IS that TF's one line."
            *   So I need to output a line for TF-2, TF-5, and any others (none shown as UNASSIGNED in the "UNITS" section except `i`, `j`, `k`, `l`, `m`? Wait).
    *   **TF-5:** Objective CAPTURE (11,1) (Enemy city with garrison).
        *   Status: Landings at t56 on enemy shore (10,1)-(12,3). Six armies ashore. Transports empty offshore. Enemy army garrisoning (11,1). No losses.
        *   Problem: TF-5 is stuck in amphibious transit/landing phase or just landed? The tasking says "CAPTURE (11,1)". The report says "LANDED t56 on the enemy shore... six armies ashore... an enemy army garrisons (11,1)".
        *   Action Needed: TF-5 has 6 armies. One is at (11,3) (`f`). Another `g` at (12,2), `h` at (12,3). The map shows water between land and the city (11,1)?
        *   Map check for (11,1): Row 0 col 6? No.
        *   Let's trace the grid visually:
            r0 . i O j . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
            r1 . k b . O . ~ ~ ~ o c E ? ?
            r2 . O . . . . ~ ~ ~ p d E g ?
            r3 . . . . O . ~ ~ ~ q e f h ?
            r4 . . . m . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
            r5 ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
        *   City (11,1) is an Enemy City ('E'). It appears at Row 1, Col 6 in my visual trace? Wait.
            Legend: `O` = my city, `E` = enemy city.
            r1 col 6 is 'c' in map text provided? No.
            Let's count spaces.
            r0: 0(.), 1(i), 2(O), 3(j), 4(.), 5(.)...
            Wait, the map string provided is: `. i O j . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?` (spaces included or not?)
            The text format usually implies columns. Let's assume space-delimited columns.
            r0: `.` (col0) `i` (1) `O` (2) `j` (3) `.` (4) `.` (5) ... This is messy.
            Let's rely on the Unit List for coordinates, they are explicit `(x,y)`.
        *   TF-5 Units:
            *   c #3 (10,1)
            *   d #4 (10,2)
            *   e #5 (10,3)
            *   f #6 (11,3)
            *   g #7 (12,2)
            *   h #8 (12,3)
            *   Transport `o` (#9) at (9,1) empty.
            *   Transport `p` (#10? No #16 is destroyer). `p` (#16 destroyers)? Wait, list says `q` (#10 destroyer). `p` (#16 transport)?
            *   List: `o` (#9 transport), `p` (#16 transport)? No.
            *   List:
                `n #18 army (2,0) UNASSIGNED` (Wait, map says city at 2,0).
                `m #17 army (3,4)`
                `l #15 army (4,3)` in city
                `k #13 army (1,1)`
                `j #12 army (3,0)` UNASSIGNED.
                `i #11 army (1,0)` UNASSIGNED.
                Wait, TF-5 has `c` `d` `e` `f` `g` `h` and transports `o`, `p`? No, look at list again:
                `o #9 transport`. `p #16 transport`. (Wait, is `p` an army or transport?)
                Line: `p  #16  army       (2,0) in city                UNASSIGNED` ?
                Let me re-read the unit list carefully.
                `o  #9   transport  (9,1) empty                  TF-5`
                `p  #16  transport  (9,2) empty                  TF-5` -> Wait, earlier I thought `q`.
                `q  #10  destroyer  (9,3) escorting              TF-5`
            *   Okay, TF-5 has transports at sea. Army c-h are on land? Or were they just off-loaded? "LANDED t56". So they are ashore near water (10,1 to 12,3).
            *   Enemy City is `(11,1)`. Map shows `E` somewhere? The text says `known enemy city (11,2)` and `X enemy army garrisoning city (11,1)`.
            *   So TF-5 has 6 armies ashore. One target is `(11,1)`.
            *   Status: "No losses". But they are "offshore" transports. The task is CAPTURE (11,1).
            *   Doctrine: "Capture attempts consume armies, and attempts fail. Assault with spares, never with exactly enough."
            *   I need to retask TF-5 to focus on capture. Since `f`, `g`, `h` are at (11,3), (12,2), (12,3). `(11,1)` is close (adjacent or 1 tile away?).
                *   (11,1) vs (11,3): same col, row diff 2. (Land unit moves on land/cities).
                *   Do they need to move? I can't order specific movements. I must RETASK the TF.
            *   If `TF-5` is CAPTURE `(11,1)`, and armies are already there ("LANDED... six armies ashore"), should I keep CAPTURE objective? Or REINFORCE UNITS?
            *   Current Tasking says "CAPTURE (11,1)". Since it's Turn 56 and they just landed (`t56`), they haven't captured yet. They need to stay in the capture role.
            *   Constraint: "Every standing TF gets exactly one line". I must update or keep TF-5 active. If I leave it as is, it implies continuation? No, the prompt asks for orders. If TF-5 status changed (captured), I wouldn't order it to continue attacking. It's currently active.
            *   Wait, `TF-2` is "DEFEND (2,0)". Units `a`, `b`. City building `ARMY` at `(2,0)`, 4 turns left. Production will soon free up an army. I should ensure TF-2 remains secure. "Defend" covers this unless there's a threat. Map shows `?` fog or sea around.
            *   `TF-5`: The transports are empty. Do they need escort? Yes, if sea is safe (they crossed). But enemy city at (11,2) implies (11,1) is garrisoned and maybe (11,2) is nearby danger. Wait, `(11,2)` is known enemy city. Is TF-5 attacking that or is the garrison there? Text: "an enemy army garrisons (11,1)". City at `(11,2)` is also Enemy.
            *   I should reinforce TF-5 with UNASSIGNED armies to attack if needed, but they have 6 already. Doctrine says "Assault with spares". If 6 are ashore, maybe I need more for reinforcement?
            *   Current status: TF-5 has armies on land near the enemy city. Transports empty offshore. `q` (destroyer) is escorting? Wait, destroyer range 3. Transport range 2.
            *   Do I need to keep TF-5 active or do something else?
            *   Actually, `TF-5` status says: "since: ... LANDED t56 on the enemy shore...". It is currently holding the beachhead. The capture hasn't happened yet. I should order them to CAPTURE `(11,1)`. But the current tasking *is* that. Do I repeat it?
            *   Instruction: "Every standing TF gets exactly one line". If the order doesn't change, do I write it? Yes, usually to confirm or update intent. But strictly speaking, if nothing changes, maybe I leave the previous order? No, I must issue amendments/orders now.
            *   If I don't write anything for a TF, does it continue unchanged? The contract says "Every standing TF gets exactly one line". I must output a line for TF-2 and TF-5.
            *   For TF-2: DEFEND is working well ("garrisoned; no contact"). I should just confirm or adjust if needed. "CONTINUE" works.
            *   For TF-5: It needs to capture the city. "CAPTURE (11,1)" is appropriate. However, they are already there. Maybe "REINFORCE UNITS"? No, I have no spare units for that yet. `i`, `j`, `k` are Unassigned at `(1,0)`, `(3,0)`, `(1,1)`. Too far away to be useful immediately? `(1,1)` is close to capital (2,0).
            *   Wait, I have 4 UNASSIGNED armies: `i` (1,0), `j` (3,0), `k` (1,1), `l` (4,3) [In City], `m` (3,4). `n` (2,0) [Unassigned but in city?].
            *   Actually, `a` is TF-2. `b` is TF-2? No, `a` and `b` are TF-2 units. `a` `(2,0)`, `b` `(2,1)`. City at (2,0).
            *   So TF-2 has `a` and `b`.
            *   My UNASSIGNED armies: `i` (1,0), `j` (3,0), `k` (1,1). `l`, `m`, `n`... wait `n` is `(2,0)` in city. Map shows `(2,0) has O my city`.
            *   Wait, Unit list says: `a  #1 army (2,0) ... TF-2`, `b  #2 army (2,1) ... TF-2`. `n` is listed as `#18 army (2,0) in city UNASSIGNED`.
            *   Map shows `O` at (2,0). `a` is inside. `b` is adjacent.
            *   There seems to be a unit `n` that I need to assign? Or `n` is a typo in my reading? Let's check the Unit List text provided in prompt again.
                `n  #18  army       (2,0) in city                UNASSIGNED`
            *   Okay, TF-2 has `a` and `b`. Unit `n` is Unassigned. This suggests TF-2 might be understaffed or `n` belongs to another group? Or I should bring `n` back?
            *   Also `l` (4,3) is in city. City `(4,3)` building ARMY, 1 turn left.
            *   My cities production: `(2,0)` building ARMY (4 turns). `(4,3)` building ARMY (1 turn).
            *   So I have armies ready at (4,3) soon. `(2,0)` will be ready in 4 turns.
            *   The priority is capturing the enemy city at (11,1). TF-5 has 6 armies ashore. That seems plenty ("Assault with spares"). But I have to keep them going or send more help?
            *   If I order "CONTINUE", it implies maintaining the mission.
            *   If I order "REINFORCE UNITS", I add units from Unassigned.
            *   Should I reinforce TF-5? It's a capture attempt. Doctrine: "Assault with spares". TF-5 already has 6 armies ashore. Is that enough? Yes, likely enough to hold and attack. Reinforcing might mean more exposure or distraction.
            *   However, TF-2 (Capital) needs support too. But `CONTINUE` is fine for TF-2 as long as it remains garrisoned.
            *   I need to check UNASSIGNED units again. `i`, `j`, `k`. Where are they? `(1,0)`, `(3,0)`, `(1,1)`. Close to capital (2,0). They could reinforce TF-2 if needed. But currently TF-5 is the main offensive.

            *   What about TF-2? Current tasking: `DEFEND (2,0)`. Do I need to change it? City building is active. It needs protection. Maybe `CONTINUE`.
            *   Are there any other tasks? Production. `(4,3)` finishes ARMY in 1 turn. I should build a transport? No, cities build ONE unit at a time. "Switching what a city builds DISCARDS all accumulated progress". I must wait.
            *   So TF-5 captures (11,1). TF-2 defends (2,0). What about the new production from (4,3) in 1 turn? Can I build there?
            *   City `(4,3)` is building an army. It will finish soon. Should I order it to build something else? No, it has a turn left on ARMY. Building another changes that progress. So do nothing on production until needed.

            *   But TF-5 is the active threat to victory.
            *   Doctrine: "A sound standing order beats a new plan." Do not change if working.
            *   TF-2: Working ("no contact"). Continue.
            *   TF-5: Active attack. Continue (or Retask? It's already CAPTURE (11,1)). The prompt asks for amendments. If I don't change it, I shouldn't output a line? No, "Every standing TF gets exactly one line". I must output lines for existing TFS to keep them active/confirmed in this turn cycle.
            *   Wait, the prompt says: "Issue your amendment orders now — ONLY the line forms defined... Every standing TF gets exactly one line". This implies I must list every *current* TF I have active? Yes.
            *   So I need lines for TF-2 and TF-5.
            *   And any other Tfs (none).
            *   Do I issue a FORM line to create a new TF for `n` (Unassigned army at 2,0)? No, that unit is already assigned to the city/capital implicitly as a defender? Or do I make it join TF-2? If I just leave it UNASSIGNED, it sits there.
            *   Actually, looking at the map: `n` is `(2,0)` in city UNASSIGNED. It shares the tile with `a`. Can't have two units on a tile unless cargo (armies only). But TF-2 has `a`. `n` is listed as UNASSIGNED army. This is slightly contradictory or implies `n` needs to be moved into TF-2?
            *   Constraint: "UNASSIGNED units enter play only through FORM or REINFORCE." So if `n` is currently on the map (in the state provided), it must be assigned to a TF already. But it says UNASSIGNED in the list. This implies the game engine keeps it unassigned until I order it.
            *   Since `n` is at `(2,0)`, which is TF-2's location, I should REINFORCE TF-2 with it.
            *   So for TF-2: `TF-2: CONTINUE | REINFORCE UNITS n`. Wait, syntax: `REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>`. The example says "TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>". I need to check if the text after is just a comment or part of the command.
            *   "Output ONLY lines in these forms — no other prose, headers, or commentary".
            *   Forms:
                `TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>`
                `TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>`
                ...
            *   This looks like the pipe `|` separates command from comment. "This is your ENTIRE force". Wait, usually prompts like this mean the first line is the order, second line is comment (ignored by parser) or just filler? "Output ONLY lines in these forms".
            *   Actually, looking at examples provided in similar tasks: `TF 1: CONTINUE | maintain position` vs `TF 1: RETASK CAPTURE 500`. The `<one line>` after the pipe is usually optional comment or explanation. But I must check "Output ONLY lines in these forms".
            *   Wait, look at this constraint again: `TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>`. This implies two parts separated by pipe. I need to fill both? Or just follow the structure? Usually `|` separates command from rationale. I will include a short rationale if possible, but "ONLY the line forms".
            *   Wait, I should check if the `|` is required in my output. Yes, the prompt lists "lines in these forms".
            *   Example format: `TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>`
            *   So I will write: `TF-2: CONTINUE | Defend capital`
            *   `TF-5: CAPTURE (11,1) | Maintain capture effort` (Current is CAPTURE). But if it's already set, do I just confirm? Yes, to keep it active for this turn.
            *   Wait, TF-5 needs the transports (`o`, `p`) and destroyer (`q`). They are in TF-5 currently. The armies `c-h` are in TF-5.
            *   Do I need to REINFORCE TF-2 with army `n`? Yes, it sits idle at the capital alongside `a`. If I want it to be effective defense, I should reinforce. Or leave it if TF-2 is sufficient. But having spare units at a garrisoned city usually implies they are just sitting there in UNASSIGNED. "UNASSIGNED units enter play only through FORM or REINFORCE." So they are not "in play" yet. If they are physically on the map, this might be game state quirk. I will assign `n` to TF-2 to integrate it into defense.
            *   Wait, unit `l` (4,3) in city is UNASSIGNED. `(4,3)` produces ARMY in 1 turn. So `l` is likely waiting for production or ready to move.
